_publications._publicationTitle: Restoring functional neurofibromin by protein transduction.

_contextSnippet: In 3 of 5 patients (NF213, NF244 and NF282) the synNF1 transduction using the Endo-Porter transduction reagent (Gene Tools) exclusively led to a distinct diminishment of the median aberration angle
details: 3 of 5 patient fibroblast lines (NF213, NF244, NF282) showed distinct improvement in orientation capability with synNF1 transduction alone; 2 lines (NF329, NF191) required additional PCI treatment for improvement
